name: kalahar_water
description: Dry Season (Water Rights), q.side_kalahar_water slot 42, end-to-end: take Well-Keeper Marisol's brief (board -> dry_brief -> dry_season_active), walk the Oasis raid - beat the Yield Officer (sq_pump_officer, ace 50), then the Site Foreman (sq_pump_foreman, ace 51, dialog-gated on the officers defeat tag), shut the manifold (shut_pump: oasis_pump_off + the -3 #idx cd_instability clawback), then report back for water_reward (750-face payout + mystic_water x2 + super_potion x2 + oasis_restored + dry_season_done) and the dry_done tombstone with the Cyber City forward hook. TWO REAL BATTLE BEATS via dev-bot autobattle (2x lv-100 Garchomp; the fights are not level-locked) - exposed to the known intermittent TBCS battle-init stall / showdown wedge (~1/10; recovery is dev showdown revive + /reloadshowdown, blind the rctapi maps first). The Oasis is a WILD zone (mobsSpawn true): day + hostile sweep + regen bracket the fights (safari_yards precedent). The instability index is pinned to 10 in the preamble so the shut-off clawback is observable above the 0 floor; payouts are instability-skewed so item counts, not balances, are the reward asserts. Re-runs accumulate two Garchomps per run past the party-emptying preamble (wheat_war caveat).

steps.comment: -- SITE NOTES: Marisols coords are an integer-authored PROPOSED placement (builder-confirm pending) - probe before first run. The Oasis crew trio carry half-block hand-adjusted placements (y 114/116, matching the live-fixed mew-wisp giver at y=114 next door) but are unconfirmed by this author, so the stand-spot is probe-listed too. Crew display names are exactly Yield Officer / Site Foreman - the wheat farms field their OWN yield-officer cast, so every named kill below carries a distance guard. --

steps.comment_: leftover-dialog guard: a prior aborted run can leave a dialog up; closing with no screen open is a no-op

steps.comment: -- reset (idempotent re-runs): every tag the chain sets, plus the recognized_late preempt (fields_liberated gte 4 flips the crew dialog to the stand-down entry), plus the reward items, plus the instability pin --

steps.comment_: recognized_late (prio 30) preempts both crew entries at fields_liberated >= 4; this scenario asserts the default + foreman_ready lane
instant band-tag removal so we do not race the band tick
dev-world global: pins the index so the shut-off clawback (-3, floored at 0) is observable; asserted 7 after the shut, reset to 0 in cleanup

steps.comment: -- tp BEFORE the kills (kill silently no-ops in unloaded chunks, then the latch re-arm dupes the body - wheat_war lesson). Crew bodies are killed + latches re-armed for a FRESH fight: the defeat tags stripped above reset the dialog lane, the respawn resets the trainer. Stand on-site through the re-arm sleep - the latch needs a player within 40b at tick time. --

steps.comment_: latch respawn fires on the ambient tick while we stand inside the 40b window

steps.comment: -- party prep: empty to one slot (a junk party stalls the TBCS battle init, wheat_war verified live), two lv-100 Garchomps for attrition headroom across both fights, autobattle armed --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon garchomp level=100
execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon garchomp level=100
execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative dev bot autobattle on

steps.comment: -- beat 1: Marisol at the dry well - the board (prio 20 preempts the default greet while oasis_restored is absent), then the dry_brief sub-dialog, then the accept. open_dialog swaps the entry in place (pay_probes precedent: click, short sleep, expect). --

expect_screen_text: Kalahar Reach. Half a town and a whole lot of dust. The wells died in one night - all of them - and the caravans went from rude to dangerous the same week.

If you are the helping kind: my wells. And ask Warden Ossa about the boundary stones - she thinks the northern fields were never the Company to sell. Word is a field already went quiet under someone out there. The grain barons did not take it well.

The Oasis, southwest of town, out in the open sand where the mobs still roam. There is a Company pump crew there running a line north to water the granary they took. They drained us to grow their wheat.
Shut their pump and the aquifer finds us again. It is a walk through live country and they will not thank you for it. But the Reach will.

steps.comment: -- beat 2: the Oasis - Yield Officer first (the crew falls in order). Fresh sweep + regen brackets the fights; battle buttons have no close action, so the stale dialog is dismissed while the fight runs server-side (wheat_war precedent) --

expect_screen_text: This is a licensed extraction site. Agricultural yield optimization, filed and verified. The water goes where the Company needs it.
The town wells are a legacy allocation under review. Your concern is noted and will be processed. Now step back from the manifold.

steps.comment_: battle-start buttons have no close action; the fight runs server-side (autobattle) while the stale dialog stays up
defeat tag + band_tags settle before the foreman gate is read

steps.comment: -- beat 3: the Site Foreman. foreman_ready (prio 25) is gated on the officers defeat tag - its say rendering IS the order-gate assert --

expect_screen_text: The officer logged you and ran. I hold the valve. Line pressure is my job and my job is filed, verified, and none of your concern.
You came all the way out here to argue with a wheel. Make them stop pumping, then - if you can get past me.

steps.comment_: battle-start buttons have no close action; the fight runs server-side (autobattle) while the stale dialog stays up

steps.comment: -- beat 4: the manifold. The ready entry (prio 20) unlocks only with BOTH crew down; shut_pump latches oasis_pump_off + claws the pinned index 10 -> 7 (title fires before the subtitle in the function, so the title is the wait) --

steps.expect_screen_text: The crew is down and the manifold is yours. A single wheel valve holds the whole diversion. Turn it and give Kalahar its aquifer back.

steps.comment: -- beat 5: report to Marisol (report_water, prio 30). water_reward fires subtitle THEN title; wait_chat is seq-cursored since the previous step, so the shut-offs earlier title cannot satisfy this second REACH DRINKS --

expect_screen_text: You are back - dust to your knees, which means you walked the Oasis. Then it is over: the line is dead and the ground is ours again.

steps.click: The pump is shut - the Reach drinks

steps.wait_chat: ADJUSTMENT: rounding, in the Company favor

steps.comment: -- the goodwill bundle: mystic_water x2 + super_potion x2, exact counts (preamble cleared both ids). The 750-face payout is instability-skewed (index rides at 7 here), so no exact balance assert --
-- beat 6: the dry_done tombstone (prio 40) + the Cyber City forward hook. No buttons on this entry - close via the driver --

expect_screen_text: You shut their pump. The well spat mud, then sand, then water. I had forgotten the sound. Whatever you are, stranger, the Reach drinks tonight because of you.
They filed our thirst as yield optimization. You filed it back. Go on to Cyber City if you are going - the caravans say the Company builds its real work in the wired town past the eastern gap.

steps.comment_: the tombstone entry has no buttons

steps.comment: -- cleanup: autobattle off, every latched tag stripped so no other scenario inherits the lane, reward items cleared, the instability pin released to the dev-world baseline 0. The two Garchomps ride in the party (wheat_war caveat); the balance rides (no reset rail). --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative dev bot autobattle off
